title: Introduction to Python Programming
description: A beginner-friendly course covering Python fundamentals: variables, control flow, functions, and data structures. Learn to think like a programmer while building real projects that serve human purpose.

lessons.title: Your First Program
objectives: Understand what a program is and how Python executes code
Use the print() function to display output
Write and run a complete Python script
hints: The print() function displays text to the console. Try: print('something')
Strings in Python are wrapped in quotes — single or double quotes both work
Look at the TODO comments in main.py for exactly what to fill in
assessmentCriteria: Output includes a statement about what the student wants to learn

lessons.title: Variables and Data Types
objectives: Create and assign variables in Python
Understand the difference between strings, integers, floats, and booleans
Use type() to inspect data types
Convert between data types with int(), str(), float()
hints: Variables are created with the = operator: name = 'Alex'
Python has four basic types: str, int, float, bool. Use type(x) to check.
To convert types: int('42') gives 42, str(42) gives '42'
assessmentCriteria: All variables are assigned with correct types
Type conversions produce the expected values
Program runs without TypeError or ValueError

lessons.title: Making Decisions with If/Else
objectives: Write if, elif, and else statements
Combine conditions with and, or, not
hints: An if statement checks a condition: if age >= 18: print('adult')
Use elif for additional conditions, else for the fallback case
Combine conditions: if age >= 13 and age < 18: print('teenager')
assessmentCriteria: Comparison operators produce correct boolean results

lessons.title: Repeating Actions with Loops
objectives: Use for loops to iterate over sequences
Use while loops for conditional repetition
Control loop flow with break and continue
Use range() to generate number sequences
hints: A for loop iterates over items: for item in [1, 2, 3]: print(item)
range(5) generates numbers 0 through 4. range(1, 6) generates 1 through 5.
Use 'break' to exit a loop early, 'continue' to skip to the next iteration
assessmentCriteria: for and while loops are used correctly
Loop termination conditions prevent infinite loops
break and continue are used appropriately

lessons.title: Writing Reusable Functions
objectives: Define functions with def and return values
Write functions with default parameter values
hints: Define a function: def add(a, b): return a + b
Default parameters: def greet(name='World'): return f'Hello, {name}'
Variables inside a function are local — they don't exist outside it
assessmentCriteria: Functions are defined with correct syntax
Return values are used instead of print inside functions
Parameters and default values work correctly
